channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding Connecticut man captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of holding her stepson captive for most of his life briefly appeared in court Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 30 years after music icon Selena's murder, Yolanda Saldívar is up for parole. Here's what to know
item.description: Thirty years ago, music legend Selena Quintanilla-Pérez was killed by her fan club’s president, Yolanda Saldívar. For the last three decades, Saldívar has served her life sentence in Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN AP Music Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration goes to Supreme Court to block teacher preparation grants
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court Wednesday to allow it to block federal teacher preparation grants that lower courts have ordered resumed.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KREX 5

item.title: US could run short of money to pay its bills by August without a debt limit deal, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is on track to hit its statutory debt ceiling — the so-called X-date when the country runs short of money to pay its bills— as early as August without a deal between lawmakers and the White House, according to a Congressional Budget Office report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amanda Knox: Prosecutor who put me in prison still texts me every day
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Sewage sludge can find a second life on farm fields. Here's how it's made
item.description: What goes down your toilet can end up on farm fields across the United States. Biosolids, or sewage sludge, are the solid byproducts of the wastewater treatment process. Rich in nutrients, they can be used as fertilizer on agricultural fields or compost on lawns.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A new Chili’s near Scranton will be a throwback to 'The Office,' 'awesome blossom' and all
item.description: It has been nearly two decades since the workplace mockumentary "The Office” first set an episode inside a Chili's, where Michael Scott handed out Dundie awards to his ever-tolerant employees, including trophies for the whitest sneakers and for stinking up the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: ICE in Boston nabs previously deported Dominican fentanyl trafficker, child rape convict, murderers
item.description: ICE agents in the Boston area have arrested previously deported fentanyl migrant traffickers, a child rape convict and several migrants wanted for murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dollar Tree selling Family Dollar to a pair of private equity firms for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree is selling Family Dollar to a pair of private equity firms for $1 billion after unsuccessfully trying to incorporate the bargain chain for a decade.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business News, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Perpetual adoration is a growing Catholic trend. A Holy Year event is scheduled for the weekend
item.description: HIALEAH, Fla. (AP) — Twice a week, Luisa Arguello and her husband spend from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. praying in the perpetual adoration chapel at St. Benedict Catholic Church, tucked on a quiet palm-fringed residential street in the Miami suburb of Hialeah. “Your body clock gets used to it.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: (KTLA) -- The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday. The team released a statement Tuesday afternoon that said it would visit the White House "in keeping with long-standing baseball tradition.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbia University student protester sues Trump admin to prevent deportation
item.description: A Columbia University student has sued President Trump and other administration officials after they attempted to revoke her permanent resident status and remove her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta Air Lines unveils 2025 trading cards to celebrate 100 years
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Another camouflaged camera found in yard of Los Angles home
item.description: This is at least the fourth hidden camera discovery in March alone, coming just days after another hidden camera was recovered after a home was burglarized.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines flight to China diverted to San Francisco after pilot forgets passport
item.description: A United Airlines flight headed to Shanghai, China, had to turn around over the weekend after the pilot forgot to bring a passport, the airline company said.
item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, KRON4

item.title: Fired federal workers face choices now that a judge wants them rehired
item.description: Sam Peterson is one of thousands of fired federal workers who was offered his job back under a judge’s order, but he didn’t jump at the chance to go back to his park ranger position at Washington state’s Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Jonas Brothers to record new theme song for Disneyland's 70th anniversary
item.description: The Jonas Brothers are bringing a bit of Disney magic to their setlist. On Sunday, the “Happiest Place on Earth” announced that the trio will record an upbeat new theme song to celebrate the resort’s upcoming 70th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC
